content: The Southern Company is a real regulated-utility issuer; the provider ratings shown here are illustrative. It is a holding company whose regulated operating subsidiaries make provider treatment of parent support and sector-specific adjustments differ materially. Both anchor providers assess Southern on the same Q3 fundamentals filed 2025-11-10, yet reach a two-notch gap: the divergence is driven by methodology (parent-support uplift versus a standalone, sector-conservative view), not by input freshness. The Southern Company is the anchor case for methodology-driven divergence.
disclaimer: Real issuer (SEC filer); the provider ratings, factors, and dates shown are illustrative for demonstration and are not actual agency ratings.
